category_title: Students with a history of school failure and deep mistrust of the adult world
scenario_title: Introducing daily novel reading to students expecting failure
minimal_context: The student is sitting in the classroom at the beginning of the autumn term as the teacher introduces the daily novel reading requirement.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Welcome to class. For our Swedish subject, we are going to read novels every day in school.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: No. I do not want to work.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Are you motivated to start reading your book today?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: No. The only thing I know that I can do is fail.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Just try reading the first chapter, and then you can write a report about what you read.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I'm not doing that. You always make us write about it. I'm so disappointed in school and the adult world, I'm just going to fail anyway.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Welcome to class. For our Swedish subject, we are going to read novels every day in school.

STUDENT: No. I do not want to work.

TUTOR: Are you motivated to start reading your book today?

STUDENT: No. The only thing I know that I can do is fail.

TUTOR: Just try reading the first chapter, and then you can write a report about what you read.

STUDENT: I'm not doing that. You always make us write about it. I'm so disappointed in school and the adult world, I'm just going to fail anyway.
